zh.lifeSummary: 當John le Strange III於1194年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾斯沃迪時，他的父親John le Strange II40歲，母親Amicia la Fusche23歲。他於大約1225年與Lucy de Tregoz在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1214年登記服兵役。他於1236年在英國斯塔福爾被列為sheriff of salopshire and stafford。他去世於1269年3月26日在英國史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔享年75歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John le Strange III于1194年出生在英国英格兰史洛普爾斯沃迪时，他的父亲John le Strange II40岁，母亲Amicia la Fusche23岁。他于大约1225年与Lucy de Tregoz在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1214年登记服兵役。他于1236年在英国斯塔福爾被列为sheriff of salopshire and stafford。他去世于1269年3月26日在英国史洛普爾可諾金 在 奧斯維斯崔享年75岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When John le Strange III was born in 1194, in Cheswardine, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John le Strange II, was 40 and his mother, Amicia la Fusche, was 23. He married Lucy de Tregoz about 1225, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He registered for military service in 1214. In 1236, his occupation is listed as sheriff of salopshire and stafford in Staffordshire, England. He died on 26 March 1269, in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, at the age of 75.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John le Strange III est né en 1194, à Cheswardine, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John le Strange II, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Amicia la Fusche, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Lucy de Tregoz environ 1225, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1214. En 1236, sa profession de son indiquée est sheriff of salopshire and stafford à Staffordshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 26 mars 1269, à Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 75 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John le Strange III 1194 in Cheswardine, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John le Strange II 40 und seine Mutter Amicia la Fusche 23. Er hat um 1225 in Shropshire, England Lucy de Tregoz geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er hat sich 1214 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1236 ist sein Beruf als sheriff of salopshire and stafford in Staffordshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 26. März 1269 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John le Strange III è nato il nel 1194 nel Cheswardine, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John le Strange II, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Amicia la Fusche, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Lucy de Tregoz circa 1225, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1214. Nel 1236, la sua occupazione è indicata come sheriff of salopshire and stafford nel Staffordshire, England. Morì il 26 marzo 1269, nel Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, all'età di 75 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John le Strange IIIが1194年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアチェスワーディンで生まれた時，父John le Strange IIは40歳，母Amicia la Fuscheは23歳であった。約1225年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでLucy de Tregozと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1214年に兵役登録をした。1236年，イギリススタフォードシュアイアでの職業はsheriff of salopshire and staffordと記録されている。彼は1269年3月26日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアノクイン イン オスウエストリーで75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John le Strange III(이)가 1194년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 체스워딘에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John le Strange II(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Amicia la Fusche(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1225년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Lucy de Tregoz(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1214년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1236년, 그의 직업은 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 sheriff of salopshire and stafford이었다. 그는 1269년 3월 26일 영국 슈럽실르 노킨 인 오스베스트리에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.Knockin, Oswestry, Shropshire, England: Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando John le Strange III nasceu em 1194, em Cheswardine, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John le Strange II, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Amicia la Fusche, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Lucy de Tregoz aproximadamente 1225, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1214. Em 1236, sua profissão aparece como sheriff of salopshire and stafford em Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 26 de março de 1269, em Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 75 anos.

ru.Knockin, Oswestry, Shropshire, England: ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John le Strange III родился в 1194 г. в местности чесвардаин, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John le Strange II было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Amicia la Fusche, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy de Tregoz около 1225 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. В 1214 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1236 годуработал по профессии sheriff of salopshire and stafford в местности стаффордшир, Англия. Он умер 26 марта 1269 г. в местности ноккин ин освестри, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 75 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John le Strange III nació en 1194, en Cheswardine, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John le Strange II, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Amicia la Fusche, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Lucy de Tregoz aproximadamente 1225, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1214. En 1236, su ocupación registrada era sheriff of salopshire and stafford en Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 26 de marzo de 1269, en Knockin in Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 75 años.
